title: Gonolobus haitiensis
intro: Gonolobus haitiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the Haitian milk vine. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gonolobus haitiensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions and infusions, as well as extracts and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive disorders and support overall health.
taxonomy: Gonolobus haitiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the Caribbean region. Gonolobus haitiensis is classified as a member of the genus Gonolobus and the tribe Asclepieae. This classification places it within the larger Apocynaceae family, which consists of over 2,000 species of flowering plants. The plant's classification is further supported by its physical characteristics and genetic analysis.
uses: Gonolobus haitiensis has been used in traditional medicine in Haiti, particularly in the treatment of various health issues.
